title: Temperature Scaling
concept: Temperature controls sampling randomness: low temperature makes text predictable/safe; high temperature makes it creative but nonsensical.

exercises.prompt: In AI language models, what does the 'temperature' setting control?

options.text: The physical heat of the server

exercises.explain: Temperature acts like a creativity dial, adjusting the balance between predictable facts and creative randomness.

cards.title: Predictable vs. Wild
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how an AI chooses its words? Sometimes it behaves like a serious professor, and other times like a wild, dreaming poet. It all comes down to a single dial called temperature!

cards.title: What is Temperature?
cards.body: Temperature is a setting in AI models that controls how random or creative the generated text is. By turning this dial up or down, we decide if the AI plays it safe or takes wild guesses.

cards.title: The Volume Knob
cards.body: Think of temperature like a volume knob on a speaker. Turn it down low, and you get clear, whisper-quiet predictability. Turn it up all the way to maximum, and the speaker rattles with wild, chaotic noise!

cards.title: Breakfast Time
cards.body:
If you ask an AI to complete the sentence: "For breakfast I ate..."
• With Low Temperature (0.2): It says "cereal and toast" (very common).
• With High Temperature (1.5): It says "spicy cardboard triangles" (creative but weird!).

cards.title: Is Higher Always Better?
cards.body: People often think setting the temperature to max makes the AI a genius. Actually, too much temperature makes the AI babble complete nonsense! Balance is key.

cards.title: The Temperature Scale
cards.body: See how temperature shifts the AI's focus between safety and chaos.

left.text: Predictable, safe, repeats the most likely words. Good for facts.

right.text: Unpredictable, creative, takes risks with unusual words. Good for stories.

exercises.prompt: If you want an AI to write a factual news report that is highly predictable and doesn't make things up, which temperature setting should you choose?
exercises.explain: Low temperature settings restrict the AI to high-probability words, making the output highly predictable and repeatable.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Setting the temperature closer to 0 makes the AI's responses more consistent and repeatable.
exercises.explain: Lowering the temperature reduces randomness, meaning the AI is much more likely to repeat the exact same output for the same prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word about AI temperature settings.
exercises.sentence: Setting a high temperature makes the AI's word choices more [blank] and creative.
exercises.explain: High temperature settings introduce more randomness, which helps the AI think outside the box but can lead to mistakes.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sentences was most likely generated by an AI with an extremely high temperature setting?

options.text: "Yesterday, I went to the park and saw a friendly dog wagging its tail."
"To build a campfire, you need dry wood, kindling, and a safe match."
"The sky bicycle whispered yellow gravity because banana socks jump tomorrow."

exercises.explain: AI outputs with very high temperature (like 1.8) often look chaotic and struggle to form grammatically correct or logical sentences.

exercises.prompt: You are setting up an AI customer service chatbot and need its answers to be highly reliable, consistent, and safe. What happens if you turn the temperature setting all the way down to 0?

options.text: It gets highly creative and uses wild vocabulary.
It becomes extremely predictable, choosing only the top words.

exercises.explain: A temperature of 0.0 makes the AI choose only the most likely next word, producing the same safe, predictable sentence every single time.
